title: androsace adfinis subsp. brigantiaca
intro: Androsace adfinis subsp. brigantiaca, also known as the Alpine rose or Rock jasmine, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Primulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its astringent and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Androsace adfinis subsp. brigantiaca include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential medicinal applications and benefits.
